_about: Scumm Bar theme (room 28 soun_001), MT-32/Munt direction. Frame copied from r010: 30 BPM type-2 iMUSE -> zenlen 192 + timer 167 + note_scale 2 (quarters as halves, 2004ms).
title: Scumm Bar
composer: M. Land / P. McConnell / C. Bajakian
_balance_note: vol_scales from tools/audio/balance.py vs build/mt32_scummbar refs. B/C and D/E are split voices: joint power match (s^2*(pB+pC)=p_ref). Drums get HALF the measured correction in dB (r010 lesson: energy match reads way too loud on one-shot kits).
_vibrato_note: MP vibrato: depth_cents,quarter_wavelength_ticks,delay_ticks. Lead 30c at ~6Hz (quarter-wl 2 ticks of 20.9ms) after 250ms delay — MT-32 flute character; per emberling comparison.
_echo_note: Bak Sangwoo room (Chad-approved A/B): NEGATIVE feedback (-86/128) phase-inverts each repeat -- no DC buildup, hollow instead of metallic, so the room can run wetter (EVOL 36 vs 24) without flutter. All percussion + bass stay dry (their echo mask).
_horn_note: bar_pad (horn) at 0.90 vs measured-MT-32 0.54: both human arrangers feature the horn near lead level (Bak v161 vs flute v170); Chad: horn forward.
_ride_note: The stiff click was the rim/side-stick (note 37): 43 hits, vel to 127, steady 0.4s ride pattern. Mapped to MT-32 cabasa (brush sweep). Claves (note 75, vel 61-80, sparse accents) was wrongly suspected first; restored to its authentic sample.
